desc_ar: إنجليزي بسيط عن العروض التقديمية: كيف تجهّز، وتتكلّم بوضوح، وتقف أمام الناس بثقة.
why_ar: لتتعلّم كلمات الإنجليزي التي تحتاجها عندما تجهّز عرضاً وتقدّمه في الجامعة أو العمل.
outcomes: تقرأ نصاً بسيطاً عن تجهيز عرض تقديمي
تتعلّم كلمات: present, slides, audience, notes
تتعلّم متى تستخدم should و have to
warmup: هل قدّمت عرضاً أمام الناس من قبل؟
ما الذي يجعل العرض جيداً في رأيك؟

grammar.explain_ar: نستخدم should للنصيحة (فكرة جيدة)، ونستخدم have to للواجب (لازم). بعد كليهما يأتي الفعل بدون to. مثال: You should practice. / You have to be on time.
grammar.explain_en_html:
should / have to

We use should to give advice — it is a good idea.
You should practice before the meeting.

We use have to for an obligation — you must do it, it is a rule.
You have to be on time.

After should and have to we use the base verb (no to).

examples.sentence: You should practice your presentation at home.
examples.translation_ar: من الأفضل أن تتدرّب على عرضك في البيت.

examples.sentence: You have to be on time for the meeting.
She has to send the slides today.

mistakes.explanation_ar: بعد should نستخدم الفعل بدون to.

mistakes.wrong: He have to be on time.
mistakes.correct: He has to be on time.
mistakes.explanation_ar: مع he/she/it نستخدم has to وليس have to.

items.question: You ___ be on time for the meeting. (rule)

readings.label: A
paragraphs: Sami is a student. On Monday he has to give a short *presentation* at his university. He is a little worried. His teacher says, "You should *prepare* well. A good *speaker* is always ready." Sami wants to do a good job, so he starts early. He makes a plan and writes his ideas on a small card.

First, Sami makes his *slides* on the computer. He puts one big *idea* on each slide. He does not put too many words. Then he writes some short *notes* to help him remember. "You should *practice* many times," his friend says. "And you have to speak *clearly*, not too fast."

On Sunday night, Sami *practices* in his room. He stands up and talks to the wall. He speaks slowly and clearly. Now he feels calm. He is ready for his *audience*. Tomorrow he will stand in front of the class and give his presentation.

questions.question_en: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: A student gets ready for a presentation
A student buys a new computer
A teacher gives a long lesson
A friend travels to another city
questions.correct_answer: A student gets ready for a presentation
questions.explanation_ar: النص كله عن سامي وهو يجهّز عرضه التقديمي.

questions.question_en: When does Sami have to give his presentation?
questions.question_ar: متى يجب على سامي أن يقدّم عرضه؟
questions.explanation_ar: قال النص إنه يجب أن يقدّم العرض يوم الاثنين.

questions.question_en: How many ideas does Sami put on each slide?
questions.question_ar: كم فكرة يضع سامي في كل شريحة؟
questions.explanation_ar: قال النص إنه يضع فكرة كبيرة واحدة في كل شريحة.

questions.question_en: In the passage, what does "clearly" mean?
choices: In a way that is easy to understand
questions.correct_answer: In a way that is easy to understand
questions.explanation_ar: يتكلّم بوضوح أي بطريقة سهلة الفهم، ببطء وليس بسرعة.

questions.question_en: Why does Sami feel calm on Sunday night?
questions.question_ar: لماذا يشعر سامي بالهدوء ليلة الأحد؟
questions.explanation_ar: شعر بالهدوء لأنه تدرّب في غرفته وأصبح جاهزاً.

questions.question_en: What kind of student is Sami?
questions.question_ar: أي نوع من الطلاب هو سامي؟
choices: He is lazy and never studies
He does not care about school
questions.explanation_ar: نفهم أنه مجتهد لأنه بدأ مبكراً وتدرّب كثيراً.

choices: He makes a plan and writes his ideas
He stands in front of the class
questions.correct_answer: He makes a plan and writes his ideas
questions.explanation_ar: في البداية عمل خطة وكتب أفكاره على بطاقة صغيرة.

vocab: a talk you give in front of people
He gave a short presentation at work.
You should prepare before the meeting.
a person who talks to people
A good speaker is always ready.
computer pages you show on a screen
He made ten slides for his talk.
He put one big idea on each slide.
short words you write to help you remember
She reads her notes before she speaks.
to do something many times to get better
in a way that is easy to understand
Please speak clearly, not too fast.
the people who watch and listen
The audience listened to the speaker.
not calm because you think of a problem
He was a little worried before the talk.

readings.label: B
paragraphs: Layla works at a small company. This week her *manager* asks her to give a *presentation* about a new plan. It is her first time. She has to speak in front of ten people. She feels a little afraid, but she wants to do well. "You should not be afraid," her manager says. "You know the plan very well."

Layla starts to *prepare* on Tuesday. She makes clean *slides* with big words and simple pictures. She writes her *goal* at the top: "Help the team save time." Then she makes short *notes* on a card. Her friend Nora helps her. "You have to *practice*," Nora says. "And you should look at the *audience*, not only at your slides."

On the day of the presentation, Layla stands up and speaks *slowly* and clearly. She looks at the people and *smiles*. She does not read every word from the card. The team likes her plan. Her manager says, "Good job! You were ready." Layla feels happy and *proud*.

questions.question_en: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Layla gives her first presentation at work
Layla looks for a new job
Layla goes on a long trip
Layla buys a new computer for the office
questions.correct_answer: Layla gives her first presentation at work
questions.explanation_ar: النص كله عن أول عرض تقديمي لليلى في العمل.

questions.question_en: How many people does Layla have to speak in front of?
questions.question_ar: أمام كم شخص يجب أن تتحدّث ليلى؟
questions.explanation_ar: قال النص إنها يجب أن تتحدّث أمام عشرة أشخاص.

questions.question_en: What does Layla write at the top of her slides?
questions.question_ar: ماذا تكتب ليلى في أعلى شرائحها؟
choices: Her goal: help the team save time
A long story about her day
questions.correct_answer: Her goal: help the team save time
questions.explanation_ar: كتبت هدفها في الأعلى: مساعدة الفريق على توفير الوقت.

questions.question_en: Why does the team like Layla's presentation?
choices: Because she was ready and spoke clearly
Because the presentation was very long
Because she gave everyone a gift
Because she did not use any slides
questions.correct_answer: Because she was ready and spoke clearly
questions.explanation_ar: أعجبهم لأنها كانت جاهزة وتحدّثت بوضوح وببطء.

questions.question_en: In the passage, what does "proud" mean?
choices: Happy because you did something well
questions.correct_answer: Happy because you did something well
questions.explanation_ar: شعرت بالفخر أي سعيدة لأنها أدّت عملها بشكل جيد.

questions.question_en: How does Layla probably feel at the start of the week?
questions.question_ar: كيف تشعر ليلى غالباً في بداية الأسبوع؟
questions.explanation_ar: نفهم أنها متوترة قليلاً لكنها تريد أن تنجح وتبدأ التجهيز.

questions.question_en: What does Layla do on the day of the presentation?
questions.question_ar: ماذا تفعل ليلى في يوم العرض؟
choices: She speaks slowly and looks at the people
She starts to make her slides
She writes her goal on a card
questions.correct_answer: She speaks slowly and looks at the people
questions.explanation_ar: في يوم العرض وقفت وتحدّثت ببطء ونظرت إلى الناس.

vocab: the person who leads a team at work
Her manager asked her to give a talk.
a talk you give in front of people
She gave a presentation about a new plan.
Layla started to prepare on Tuesday.
computer pages you show on a screen
She made clean slides with big words.
the thing you want to reach
Her goal was to help the team save time.
short words you write to help you remember
She made short notes on a card.
to do something many times to get better
You have to practice before the talk.
the people who watch and listen
Look at the audience when you speak.
happy because you did something well
Layla felt happy and proud after the talk.

listening.transcript: Hi, I'm Omar. On Wednesday I have to give a presentation at work. I feel a little worried, so I prepare early. First, I make simple slides with big words. Then I write short notes on a card. My friend says I should practice many times and speak clearly. Tonight I will practice in my room. I want to be ready for my audience.

exercises.question_en: When does Omar have to give his presentation?
exercises.explanation_ar: قال عمر إنه يجب أن يقدّم عرضه يوم الأربعاء.

exercises.question_en: Where does Omar have to give his presentation?
exercises.explanation_ar: قال إنه يجب أن يقدّم العرض في العمل.

exercises.question_en: What does Omar put on his slides?
What does Omar's friend say he should do?
exercises.explanation_ar: قال صديقه إنه يجب أن يتدرّب كثيراً ويتكلّم بوضوح.

options: Because he feels a little worried
exercises.explanation_ar: يجهّز مبكراً لأنه يشعر بالقلق قليلاً.

exercises.question_en: What is the main idea of the talk?
options: Omar is getting ready for a presentation
Omar is going on a trip
Omar is buying a new phone
Omar is looking for a job
exercises.explanation_ar: الفكرة الرئيسية أن عمر يستعدّ لتقديم عرض.

writing.title_en: Write a short plan for your presentation
writing.prompt_en: Write a short plan for a presentation. What is your topic? What should you do to prepare? Use should and have to.
writing.prompt_ar: اكتب خطة قصيرة لعرض تقديمي. ما هو موضوعك؟ ماذا يجب أن تفعل للاستعداد؟ استخدم should و have to.
hints: I should speak clearly and look at the audience.

speaking.prompt_en: Talk about a presentation. What is your topic? What should you do to prepare? What do you have to remember?
speaking.prompt_ar: تحدّث عن عرض تقديمي. ما هو موضوعك؟ ماذا يجب أن تفعل للاستعداد؟ ماذا يجب أن تتذكّر؟
prep: حضّر كلمات: present, slides, audience, practice
رتّب أفكارك: الموضوع، التجهيز، ثم النصيحة
